nodes.label: FAA Regulations
nodes.description: Federal Aviation Administration laser regulations for aviation safety

nodes.label: Visual Interference Hazards
nodes.description: Three types: distraction, glare, and flashblindness

nodes.label: Nominal Ocular Hazard Distance
nodes.description: Distance at which laser irradiance falls below Maximum Permissible Exposure

nodes.label: Laser Free Zone (LFZ)
nodes.description: Airspace <2 NM from runway, <2,000 ft AGL with <50 μW/cm² limit

nodes.label: Critical Flight Zone (CFZ)
nodes.description: 10 NM radius from airport, up to 10,000 ft AGL

nodes.label: Sensitive Flight Zone (SFZ)
nodes.description: Specific airspace requiring lower exposure levels

nodes.label: Normal Flight Zone (NFZ)
nodes.description: All other airspace outside LFZ, CFZ, and SFZ

nodes.label: 18 USC 39A
nodes.description: Federal law making it a crime to aim lasers at aircraft

nodes.label: FAA AC 70-1B
nodes.description: Advisory Circular for outdoor laser operations

nodes.label: FAA AC 70-2A
nodes.description: Advisory Circular for reporting laser illumination of aircraft

nodes.label: FAA Form 7140-1
nodes.description: Notice of Proposed Outdoor Laser Operation

nodes.label: NOTAM
nodes.description: Notice to Air Missions for laser operations

nodes.label: Laser Protective Eyewear
nodes.description: OD-rated glasses for pilots in laser-prone areas

nodes.label: Green Laser (532nm)
nodes.description: Most common in incidents due to high visibility

nodes.label: ATC Laser Procedures
nodes.description: Emergency response and broadcast protocols

nodes.label: Domestic Events Network (DEN)
nodes.description: Real-time security information sharing network

nodes.label: Flashblindness
nodes.description: Vision-impairing afterimages lasting seconds to minutes

nodes.label: Glare
nodes.description: Inability to see past the laser light while it is on

nodes.label: Distraction
nodes.description: Mental interference from bright light

nodes.label: Critical Flight Phases
nodes.description: Takeoff, landing, low-level flying, emergencies
